title: FilletFeatureInput Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that pertain to the definition of a fillet feature.

methods.name: addChordLengthEdgeSet
methods.description: **RETIRED** Adds a set of edges with a chord length to this input.

methods.name: addConstantRadiusEdgeSet
methods.description: **RETIRED** Adds a set of edges with a constant radius to this input.

methods.name: addVariableRadiusEdgeSet
methods.description: **RETIRED** Adds a single edge or set of tangent edges along with variable radius information to this input.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: edgeSetInputs
properties.description: Gets the FilletEdgeSetInputs object that provides support to create the various types of edge sets that will be used to create the fillet.

properties.name: isG2
properties.description: **RETIRED** Gets and sets if the fillet uses the G2 (curvature-continuity) surface quality option.

properties.name: isRollingBallCorner
properties.description: Gets and sets if a rolling ball or setback solution is to be used in any corners. For an asymmetric fillet only a setback solution is supported, so any asymmetric edge sets will ignore this setting and will always be a setback corner.

properties.name: isTangentChain
properties.description: **RETIRED** Gets and sets if any edges that are tangentially connected to any of filleted edges will also be included in the fillet.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: targetBaseFeature
properties.description: When creating a feature that is owned by a base feature, set this property to the base feature you want to associate the new feature with. By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base feature.   Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a feature associated with a base feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base feature. The base feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

related_samples.name: Fillet Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating fillets using the various types of fillets. Create a new design and add a box that is at least 2 cm on each side. The script creates a constant radius, variable radius and chord length fillets. After creating each one, it deletes it and then creates the next. To see any of the fillets that were deleted, undo to get the fillet back.

addChordLengthEdgeSet.title: FilletFeatureInput.addChordLengthEdgeSet Method
addChordLengthEdgeSet.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Adds a set of edges with a chord length to this input. Remarks
addChordLengthEdgeSet.return_description: Returns true if the set of edges was successfully added to the FilletFeatureInput.

parameters.name: edges
parameters.description: An ObjectCollection containing the edges to be filleted. If the isTangentChain argument is true additional edges may also get filleted if they are tangentially connected to any of the input edges.

parameters.name: chordLength
parameters.description: A ValueInput object that defines the chord length of the fillet. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in") or if no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length.

parameters.name: isTangentChain
parameters.description: A boolean value for setting whether or not edges that are tangentially connected to the input edges or faces will also be filleted.

addConstantRadiusEdgeSet.title: FilletFeatureInput.addConstantRadiusEdgeSet Method
addConstantRadiusEdgeSet.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Adds a set of edges with a constant radius to this input. Remarks
addConstantRadiusEdgeSet.return_description: Returns true if the set of edges was successfully added to the FilletFeatureInput.

parameters.name: edges
parameters.description: An ObjectCollection containing the edges to be filleted. If the isTangentChain argument is true additional edges may also get filleted if they are tangentially connected to any of the input edges.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: A ValueInput object that defines the radius of the fillet. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in") or if no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length.

parameters.name: isTangentChain
parameters.description: A boolean value for setting whether or not edges that are tangentially connected to the input edges will also be filleted.

addVariableRadiusEdgeSet.title: FilletFeatureInput.addVariableRadiusEdgeSet Method
addVariableRadiusEdgeSet.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Adds a single edge or set of tangent edges along with variable radius information to this input. Remarks
addVariableRadiusEdgeSet.return_description: Returns true if the edge set was successfully added to the FilletFeatureInput.

parameters.name: tangentEdges
parameters.description: An object collection containing a single edge or multiple edges. Multiple edges must be tangentially connected and added to the collection in order.

parameters.name: startRadius
parameters.description: A ValueInput object that defines the starting radius of the fillet. If a single edge is being filleted, the start radius is at the start end of the edge. If multiple tangent edges are being filleted the start radius is the open end of the first edge in the collection.   If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in") or if no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length.

parameters.name: endRadius
parameters.description: A ValueInput object that defines the ending radius of the fillet. If a single edge is being filleted, the end radius is at the end of the edge. If multiple tangent edges are being filleted the end radius is the open end of the last edge in the collection. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in") or if no units are specified it is interpreted using the current default units for length.

parameters.name: positions
parameters.description: An array of ValueInput objects that defines the positions of any additional radii along the edge(s). The value must be between 0 and 1 and defines the percentage along the curve where a radius is defined. The value is unitless. This array must have the same number of values as the array passed in for the radii argument.

parameters.name: radii
parameters.description: An array of ValueInput objects that define the radii at positions along the edge(s). This array must have the same number of values as the array passed in for the positions argument. If the ValueInput uses a real then it is interpreted as centimeters. If it is a string then the units can be defined as part of the string (i.e. "2 in"). If no units are specified it will be interpreted using the current default units for length.

classType.title: FilletFeatureInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

edgeSetInputs.title: FilletFeatureInput.edgeSetInputs Property
edgeSetInputs.description: Gets the FilletEdgeSetInputs object that provides support to create the various types of edge sets that will be used to create the fillet.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = filletFeatureInput_var.edgeSetInputs
